tool_args.label: Capital Program Committee Meeting - May 2022
tool_args.raw_text: Capital Program Committee Meeting - May 2022. Meeting document covering Capital Program Committee sessions on April 25, 2022 and scheduled May 23, 2022, including project updates on Penn Station Access, LIRR Concourse, East Side Access, LIRR Main Line Expansion, CBTC programs, procurement actions, safety reports, and capital program commitments/completions.

payload.title: Capital Program Committee Meeting
payload.description: Capital Program Committee Meeting book for May 23, 2022, including minutes from April 25, 2022 meeting, integrated project updates, safety report, capital program status, and procurement actions.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Capital Program Committee Meeting
May 2022
Metropolitan Transportation Authority

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access - May 2022 Update
tool_args.raw_text: Penn Station Access (PSA/CBX001) - will provide Metro-North New Haven Line customers with service into and out of Penn Station via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line. Includes four new ADA-accessible stations in the East Bronx. Budget $2,482M ($2,867M including options). Substantial Completion March 2027, Closeout June 2027.

payload.description: Provides Metro-North New Haven Line customers with service into and out of Penn Station via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line. Includes four new ADA-accessible stations in the East Bronx, bridge rehabilitations, almost 20 miles of new and rehabilitated track work, new and reconfigured interlockings, modernization of signal, power and communication infrastructure.

evidence_refs.source_quote: PSA will provide Metro-North New Haven Line customers with service into and out of Penn Station via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line
PROJECT STATUS Original Forecast Substantial Completion March 2027 March 2027 Closeout June 2027 June 2027 Budget $2,482M * $2,867M *

issues: evidence_refs[1].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: LIRR Concourse - May 2022 Update
tool_args.raw_text: LIRR Concourse - widening the 33rd Street corridor at Penn Station from 30 feet to 57 feet, raising ceiling to 18 feet. Budget $538.7M. 65% complete. Scheduled to open in Q1 2023 with incentive target for Q4 2022.

payload.description: Widening the 33rd Street corridor in Penn Station from 30 feet to 57 feet, raising ceiling to 18 feet, adding passenger amenities, wayfinding, fresh air systems, and ADA improvements.

evidence_refs.source_quote: widening the corridor to 57 feet and raise the ceiling to 18 feet
PROJECT STATUS Original Forecast Substantial Completion March 2023 March 2023 Budget* $538.7 M $538.7 M
LIRR Concourse project is on schedule and budget and is scheduled to open in Q1 2023

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: East Side Access - May 2022 Update
tool_args.raw_text: East Side Access (ESA) - provides LIRR passengers with direct service to Grand Central Terminal. $11.1B budget, approximately 94% complete. Revenue Service Date in Q4 2022.

payload.description: Provides LIRR passengers with direct service to Grand Central Terminal. 11.5 mile underground tunnel with new station and concourse below existing Grand Central Terminal.

evidence_refs.source_quote: East Side Access (ESA) will provide LIRR passengers with direct service to Grand Central Terminal
PROJECT STATUS Original Forecast Revenue Service Dec 2022 Dec 2022 Budget $11.1B* $11.1B
project is currently approximately 94% complete

tool_args.label: LIRR Main Line Expansion - May 2022 Update
tool_args.raw_text: LIRR Main Line Expansion (Third Track) - adds a 10-mile-long third track on the LIRR Main Line. Budget $2,488M ($2,588M original). Approximately 87% complete. Substantial completion April 2023.

payload.description: Adds a 10-mile-long third track on the LIRR Main Line between Floral Park and Hicksville to reduce train congestion and enable bi-directional service during peak hours. Includes elimination of eight grade crossings, rail bridge improvements, reconstruction of five stations, and new parking garages.

evidence_refs.source_quote: add a 10-mile-long third track on the LIRR Main Line
PROJECT STATUS Original Forecast Substantial Completion April 2023 April 2023 Budget $2,588 M $2,488 M The project is approximately 87% complete.
elimination of eight street level grade crossings

tool_args.label: 8th Avenue CBTC - May 2022 Update
tool_args.raw_text: 8th Avenue CBTC (59th Street in Manhattan through High Street in Brooklyn) - 57% complete, on schedule with completion planned in January 2025. EAC $774M, a $14M increase. First project to feature axle counters.

payload.description: CBTC installation on the 8th Avenue Line from 59th Street in Manhattan through High Street in Brooklyn. First project to feature axle counters, replacing track circuits.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 8 th Avenue CBTC (59 th Street in Manhattan through High Street in Brooklyn), is the first project to feature axle counters
project is 57% complete and remains on schedule with completion planned in January 2025
